hook.text: Ancient glacial scratches in the Sahara Desert and coal deposits in Antarctica. Continents clearly moved.

buildup.text: Glacial striations in Africa and India prove those lands once sat near the South Pole, covered in ice sheets.

discovery.text: Coal forms in tropical swamps. Coal in Antarctica proves it was once warm and forested, near the equator.

twist.text: These climate mismatches make no sense on a static Earth. They only work if continents drifted over climate zones.

climax.text: Paleoclimate data independently confirms plate motion. The climate evidence matches the geologic evidence perfectly.

punchline.text: Deserts remember their ice and poles remember warmth.

quiz.question: What does coal in Antarctica prove?
options: Antarctica was once near the equator with tropical swamps
Coal can form in freezing conditions
